methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA represents institutional infrastructure for distributed capability-building beyond the NoVA corridor, signaling peer-to-peer education networks that could democratize deep AI fluency across Richmond's workforce.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - wdbj7.com
signals.signal_reading: An AI trainer emphasizing worker agency over technology ('lead, not follow') in Southwest Virginia signals grassroots capability development in a historically underserved region, pointing toward distributed depth rather than passive platform adoption.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Broad business embrace of AI in Hampton Roads suggests platform-driven adoption across sectors, but without evidence of deep technical capability development, this signals shallow distribution through turnkey solutions rather than indigenous expertise.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce without corresponding upskilling infrastructure suggests displacement rather than empowerment, signaling neither deep capability development nor equitable distribution of AI benefits.

signals.title: Virginia’s AI Data Center Gravy Train Shows Signs of Slowing - Bloomberg Law News
signals.signal_reading: Data center growth slowing in NoVA while concentration remains extreme reinforces the metro hub lock-in dynamic—capital and infrastructure remain concentrated even as expansion rates moderate, deepening the geographic capability gap.
